_comment: Internal all-hands deck for Northwind Holdings (DEMO). Synthetic data, not real Tiny figures. Tiny house style, friendlier internal tone.
title: Where We Stand — All-Hands April 2026

slides.title: Where We Stand
slides.subtitle: Our monthly scoreboard — the wins, the hard parts, and where we go next. Straight from the team, for the team.

slides.title: The Scoreboard
commentary: A strong month across the board. Revenue and EBITDA both up, margin expanding, and the team keeps growing — eight months of headcount growth and counting.

slides.title: Tracking vs Plan
commentary: Revenue ahead of plan by $0.7M — the Acme and Globex teams carried the month.
EBITDA ahead of plan by $0.3M as we held costs flat while revenue climbed.
Margin two points above plan — the operating discipline is paying off.
slides.note: Marker shows the monthly plan target. Figures are illustrative (DEMO).

slides.title: Wins This Month
slides.lead: A lot to celebrate this month — here's what each team pulled off.
bullets: Acme shipped the new onboarding flow and saw signups jump 22% in two weeks.
Globex closed its biggest enterprise deal ever — $1.2M ARR, a year in the making.
Initech cut support response time in half after the help-desk revamp.
Stark launched the mobile app to a 4.8-star store rating out of the gate.
Hooli hit 99.98% uptime — the best quarter on record for the platform.
Umbrella renewed three at-risk accounts and turned one into a case study.

slides.title: An Honest Look
slides.lead: We share the wins, so we share the hard parts too. Here's what we're wrestling with.
bullets: Creative Market churn ticked up — we lost two mid-size accounts and we don't fully understand why yet.
Hiring is slower than we'd like — three key engineering roles have been open for over 60 days.
Umbrella's roadmap slipped a quarter; we under-scoped the migration and it's costing us focus.
Support load is climbing faster than headcount — the team is stretched and we feel it.
Cash is healthy but margin pressure is real — cloud costs grew faster than revenue this month.
slides.footnote: We'd rather name these now than pretend they don't exist. Bring your ideas to the Q&A.

slides.title: Priorities
slides.lead: Where we're putting our energy over the next few weeks.
bullets: Crack the churn question — a small task force digs into why accounts are leaving and ships fixes fast.
Close the open roles — referral bonus doubled, and every hiring manager gets recruiter support.
Re-scope the Umbrella migration — honest timeline, smaller milestones, weekly check-ins.
Get ahead of support — two new hires plus a self-serve knowledge base launching mid-May.
Tame cloud spend — a focused week on the biggest cost drivers before they compound.
slides.footnote: Forward-looking and subject to change — but this is the plan today.

slides.title: Onward.
slides.subtitle: Proud of this team. Let's keep building.
slides.contact: Townhall Q&A — Thursday 11AM, Main Room & Zoom
